headline: Disability accessibility compliance below 30% for public buildings
context: A 2025 JKM audit found that less than 30% of public buildings in Malaysia comply with Persons with Disabilities Act accessibility requirements.

cards.big: Less than 30% of public buildings meet disability accessibility standards
cards.sub: 17 years after Persons with Disabilities Act 2008, compliance remains low

cards.big: Uniform Building By-Laws 1984 require barrier-free design for public buildings
cards.sub: Enforcement falls across local authorities with no centralised monitoring

cards.big: CRPD Article 9 requires states to ensure accessibility in all areas of life
cards.sub: Malaysia's CRPD reporting is overdue by 4 years as of 2025

cards.big: 4.8 million persons with disabilities face daily barriers in 70% of public spaces
cards.sub: Public transport accessibility rated as poor by 89% of wheelchair users surveyed

cards.big: 30% compliance after 17 years means the law exists without political commitment
MWFCD launched National Disability Action Plan 2025-2030 in January
